channel.title: Authors on Stack Insider
channel.description: Recent content in Authors on Stack Insider

item.title: David Park
item.description:
David Park is the senior editor at StackInsider, responsible for maintaining the publication’s editorial standards. Every review published on StackInsider passes through David’s desk for a final quality check — ensuring factual accuracy, structural clarity, and adherence to the site’s no-pay-to-play editorial policy.

Background

David brings 15+ years of technology publishing experience to StackInsider. Before joining, he served as managing editor at two major B2B tech publications, overseeing editorial teams of 10+ writers and managing content pipelines producing 50+ articles per month. He has edited over 5,000 technology articles spanning enterprise software, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and developer tools.

Expertise

  • Editorial Quality: Multi-stage review process ensuring every article meets professional publishing standards
  • Fact-Checking: Systematic verification of claims, pricing data, and feature comparisons against primary sources
  • Content Strategy: Developing editorial calendars, content formats, and review methodologies
  • Technical Clarity: Translating complex technical concepts into accessible, actionable prose

Editorial Standards

David developed StackInsider’s three-stage editorial review process: practitioner-written first drafts, peer review by a second domain expert, and final editorial polish. This process ensures that every review combines deep domain expertise with professional publishing quality.

item.title: Gufei.Sun
item.description:
Gufei.Sun is the founder and lead analyst at StackInsider. With over a decade of hands-on experience implementing and evaluating enterprise software at Fortune 500 companies and mid-market firms alike, he brings a practitioner’s lens to every review.

Background

Before founding StackInsider, Gufei spent 8 years as an enterprise solutions architect, leading ERP and CRM deployments across manufacturing, retail, and professional services verticals. He has managed software selection processes with budgets ranging from $50K to $5M, giving him firsthand insight into the gap between vendor promises and real-world implementation.

Expertise

  • ERP Systems: Deep knowledge of SAP, Oracle NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics 365, and Acumatica
  • CRM Software: Extensive experience with Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM, and Pipedrive
  • TCO Analysis: Rigorous methodology for calculating true total cost of ownership, including hidden implementation, training, and integration costs
  • Vendor Negotiation: Insider perspective on pricing models, discount structures, and contract pitfalls

Methodology

Every review on StackInsider follows a structured evaluation framework that Gufei developed over years of real-world software procurement. The framework scores products across six dimensions: functional depth, usability, pricing transparency, integration capability, vendor support quality, and scalability.

Connect with Gufei on LinkedIn.

item.title: Sarah Okafor
item.description:
Sarah Okafor is an industry analyst at StackInsider, where she leads market intelligence and competitive landscape research. Her work ensures that every review is contextualized within broader market dynamics — not just feature comparisons, but an understanding of where each product fits in the ecosystem.

Background

Sarah spent 6 years as a market research analyst at a leading B2B technology research firm, where she authored over 200 market reports covering SaaS, cloud infrastructure, and enterprise applications. She has tracked vendor market share shifts, pricing model evolutions, and M&A activity across the CRM, ERP, and project management sectors.

Expertise

  • SaaS Market Tracking: Monitoring vendor funding rounds, product launches, and strategic pivots
  • Pricing Trend Analysis: Tracking how SaaS pricing models evolve — per-user to usage-based, freemium expansion, and platform plays
  • Competitive Intelligence: Mapping feature parity matrices and identifying differentiated capabilities
  • Buyer Persona Research: Understanding how different organizational profiles evaluate and adopt software

Contributions

Sarah’s market context sections in StackInsider reviews help readers understand not just whether a tool is good, but whether it’s gaining or losing ground, how its pricing stacks up against the market average, and what its roadmap signals about long-term viability.
